programme.title: Interview With a Killer
programme.desc: In 2013, the small city of Weatherford, Texas was rocked by the brutal slaying of local son, JD Doss. The murder weapon? A pickaxe. The murderer? His close friend, 24-year-old Nicholas Camfield. In a shocking twist, Camfield not only killed his victim by repeatedly striking him in the head, he also mutilated, scalped, and defiled his body over the course of days. In one of David Scott's most shocking interviews, Camfield gives a glimpse inside his deranged, murderous mind.

programme.title: Interview With a Killer
programme.sub-title: The Golden Boy - Tommy Gilbert, Jr.
programme.desc: In his first ever television interview, killer Thomas Gilbert, Jr. discusses the murder that shook New York's elite. The Princeton-educated Gilbert became a tabloid obsession when he shot his father after his $1,000 allowance was cut off. But in this unique installment of Interview With a Killer, Gilbert appears to be a far cry from his once golden upbringing. Host David Scott explores his long history of mental illness and the privileged pressure cooker that shaped his downfall.

programme.title: Interview With a Killer
programme.desc: She's one of the most camera-friendly killers in true crime history.

programme.title: Interview With a Killer
programme.desc: When flames tore through a Buffalo, New York home in 2018, the investigation revealed a toxic relationship and a hot-tempered arsonist, Frank Bredt, Jr. Now Bredt is talking about his crime from behind bars. In this episode of Interview With a Killer, host David Scott revisits the fire, the evidence, and the unraveling timeline - exposing a jilted lover hidden behind smoke and destruction and giving viewers an unfiltered look into the chilling death of an innocent mother.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The FBI began an undercover operation to expose the Chicago Mafia's infiltration into the city's surrounding suburbs. The investigation resulted in the arrests of former police chief Michael J. Corbitt and Alan Masters for murder and racketeering.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1992, a young mother was gunned down in front of her children and police were left baffled. The crime seemed unmotivated and random. But family members hinted that the victim, who had been married to a prominent attorney, harbored a dark secret.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A teenage immigrant came to the United States to realize her dream of being a doctor but she found herself an innocent victim of drug trafficking. Agents discovered a trail of drugs, hit men and murder as they attempted to solve her kidnapping.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When an 80-year-old woman is found barely conscious next to the body of her murdered son in an abandoned van, the FBI was brought in and linked the crime to a teenager and middle-aged man.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After two brothers abduct and murder bank president Dan Short, FBI agents slowly piece together tiny shreds of evidence. Eventually, they're able to convict the Agofsky brothers for the 1989 Noel, Missouri bank robbery.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The bodies of several women began turning up around Vienna, Austria. The FBI and Austrian Federal Police investigated suspected serial killer Jack Unterweger, who became an international outlaw, hopping from country to country to evade capture.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves in the Alaskan wilderness. In one of the first cases of successful criminal investigative analysis, FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind and captured the serial killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1996, a group of political extremists robbed a bank in Washington using homemade bombs and military precision to escape. The FBI learned that the robbers might be preparing for a small-scale war with law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: For two couples on vacation, an island paradise turned to hell when one couple murders the other. Eleven years after the murder, a drifter sailing the high seas to escape a drug charge was convicted of the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A pair of convicted felons escaped the custody of a local deputy and fled across the country in a wave of abductions and car thefts. When a foreign military officer disappeared along the trail, the FBI believed the pair were growing more violent.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Posing as a fashion photographer, serial killer Christopher Wilder (aka "The Beauty Queen Killer") lured young women from shopping malls with promises of fame and fortune. Most ended up dead.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a career criminal committed a vehicular homicide in New Orleans, the FBI tracked him through his habits.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A teenager was shot to death on a hunting ground in Virginia. His death was initially considered an accident but investigators soon suspected foul play. Years later, a man contacted the FBI with a complex story of conspiracy and murder-for-hire.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The decapitation of an African-American male led the Miami-Dade police and the FBI along a trail of murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A bank vault was blasted into from the ceiling, and over $2 million in valuables was missing. Agents were able to track down one of the robbers' friends and attempted to use him as bait to reel in the crooks.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 2000, the FBI was overwhelmed with investigations in Seattle which had the third-highest number of bank robberies in the nation. Agents focused on a series of heists in which two heavily armed men seemed to know where the banks stored their money.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A cross-country crime spree over a Labor Day weekend terrorized the nation. In a small Ohio community, an elderly woman and teenage boy disappeared within hours of one another. The police suspected the cases were linked.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the man known as the 'Babes in the Wood Killer,' who evaded justice for thirty years.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Teen Mhairi was killed on her walk home after a festive night. Investigators traced her last steps, using CCTV and forensics that pointed to Gavin, a violent repeat offender whose history revealed how dangerous he truly was.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Goudeau terrorized victims in the Phoenix metro area between August 2005 and June 2006. In addition to committing nine murders, his extensive crime spree included 84 other felony crimes, totaling 93 felonies over the ten-month period.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.desc: At 18, Christa Pike commits a brutal murder and becomes one of the youngest ever women sent to death row.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man tells police he shot an intruder who killed his wife. Forensic evidence disproves that story later on.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1981, New York correctional officer Donna Payant disappeared and was later found in a landfill. The medical examiner not only identified the cause of death but also found an important clue.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A fan of murder mysteries and forensic science shows uses what she learned from TV to determine who was responsible for the accident which permanently injured her 4-year-old daughter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young couple decided to celebrate their first wedding anniversary with a camping trip to Mount Hood. During the trip, the husband was shot and killed. Later, his wife told two different versions of the events which led to his death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The prime suspect had a criminal record and his driver's license was found at the scene of a brutal double homicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A beverage company pleads guilty to violating federal safety laws when a baby dies from E. coli after drinking apple juice.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When hunters found a skull in a Texas canyon, police found bits of clothing, small bones and a strand of hair. A forensic artist reconstructed her face. Now all they had to do was find her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An 11-year-old Boy Scout comes down with a fever and diarrhea after eating an undercooked burger. When his condition worsens, his mother drives him to a hospital and doctors discover E. coli destroying his body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1985, 121 people in South Dakota and Minnesota were struck by a mysterious illness. There had been only one outbreak like it, and when it happened again no one could figure out why.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman's death in Pennsylvania triggers a homicide investigation into another woman's death in North Carolina.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man was killed in a car crash, but the evidence led investigators to believe it was not an accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A fireman died from what appeared to be heart disease, but his family was skeptical of the cause of death.

programme.desc: Police don't have a basis for comparison to match shoe impressions, blood drops and DNA evidence from a murder scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A beverage company pleads guilty to violating federal safety laws when a baby dies from E. coli after drinking apple juice.

programme.title: Forensic Files
